title: vicia narbonensis subsp. serratifolia
intro: Vicia narbonensis subsp. serratifolia, also known as French or Bitter vetch, is a plant species in the legume family Fabaceae, native to southern Europe and northwestern Africa. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its use as a laxative and diuretic, as well as its ability to relieve digestive issues such as bloating and gas. The health benefits of Vicia narbonensis subsp. serratifolia include its potential to lower cholesterol and blood pressure, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and improve gut health. Herbal preparations of Vicia narbonensis subsp. serratifolia can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being and support the digestive system.
